question: Using FY2025 Connectivity segment figures, calculate YoY growth in revenue, operating income, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA. What do the differential growth rates imply about operating leverage within Connectivity?

core_facts.fact: Connectivity segment revenue was $11,387 million in FY2025.
Connectivity segment revenue was $7,599 million in FY2024.
Connectivity segment revenue grew 49.85% YoY.
Connectivity segment operating income was $4,423 million in FY2025.
Connectivity segment operating income was $2,006 million in FY2024.
Connectivity segment operating income grew 120.49% YoY.
Connectivity segment Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $7,168 million in FY2025.
Connectivity segment Adjusted EBITDA was $3,849 million in FY2024.
Connectivity segment Adjusted EBITDA grew 86.23% YoY.
The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under common-control accounting.
The historical financials have been retrospectively recast to include xAI and X Holdings for all periods presented.
The segment figures reflect the recast consolidated entity, not standalone SpaceX.
The Connectivity segment is primarily Starlink.
xAI merged on February 2, 2026.
X Holdings merged into xAI on March 28, 2025.
FY 2024 Connectivity segment operating income included a $39 million impairment charge.
Connectivity segment impairment was $0 million in FY 2025.

question: Estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts. State all assumptions and identify what the filing’s subscriber and revenue definitions exclude from this ARPU estimate.

core_facts.fact: Q1 2026 Connectivity Segment Revenue was $3,257 million.
Q1 2026 Consumer Revenue was $2,148 million.
Q1 2026 Enterprise & Government revenue was $1,109 million.
Q1 2026 Products Revenue (Starlink Kit sales) was $380 million.
Starlink Subscribers as of March 31, 2026 were 10.3 million.
Starlink had 8.9 million subscribers as of December 31, 2025.
Consumer revenue includes both recurring service fees and one-time Starlink Kit (terminal) hardware sales.
A Starlink Subscriber is defined as a unique Service Line assigned to a Starlink.com account that does not have a direct, negotiated agreement with the Starlink sales team.
The 'Starlink Subscribers' metric counts unique Service Lines, not individual end users.
Managed enterprise and government customers with negotiated contracts in aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, and government entities are excluded from the Starlink Subscriber count.
Government/Starshield customers under long-term, multi-year contracts are excluded from the Starlink Subscriber count.
Individual end users sharing one Service Line are counted as one Starlink subscriber.
Enterprise & Government customers (managed enterprise, government, Starshield) are not counted in the 10.3 million Starlink subscriber base.
The SpaceX S-1 Registration Statement was filed on May 20, 2026.
Starlink Subscriber ARPU for Q1 2026 is $66 per month.
The ARPU numerator consists solely of recurring service revenue from the self-service Starlink Subscriber base.
Product revenue is excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU numerator.
Enterprise & Government service revenue comes from aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, and Starshield contracts.

question: Calculate AI and Connectivity segment capex intensity for FY2025 and Q1 2026. Compare quarter versus full-year intensity and assess what the change implies about the pace and stage of AI infrastructure buildout.

core_facts.fact: AI Segment revenue was $727M in Q1 2025.
AI segment FY2025 revenue was $3,201M.
AI segment Q1 2026 revenue was $818M
Connectivity Segment FY2025 revenue was $11,387M
Connectivity segment Q1 2026 revenue was $3,257M.
AI Segment capital expenditures were $2,567M in Q1 2025.
AI segment capital expenditures were $12,727M in FY2025
AI segment capital expenditures were $7,723M in Q1 2026.
Connectivity Segment capital expenditures were $4,178M in FY2025.
Connectivity Segment capital expenditures were $1,332M in Q1 2026.
The company expects a multi-year investment horizon before deployments translate into sustained positive AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA.
SpaceX filed an S-1 on May 20, 2026.
All figures were retrospectively recast under common-control accounting to include xAI and X Holdings for all periods.
